concrete_cover.title: Nominal Concrete Cover for Durability
concrete_cover.description: Minimum clear distance from concrete surface to outermost reinforcement bar to protect steel from environmental ingress.
concrete_cover.keywords: concrete cover clear durability corrosion crack width water tank bridge marine

standards.limits:
Mild: 20mm | Moderate: 30mm | Severe: 45mm | Very Severe: 50mm | Extreme: 75mm.
Note: Liquid-retaining faces in IS 3370 require a flat minimum of 45mm cover.
standards.notation:
**c_nom**: Nominal cover on drawings
**c_min**: Minimum required cover for exposure class
**Δc_dev**: Execution deviation tolerance (typically 0mm in design office drawings).

**c_nom**: Total nominal cover
**c_min,dur**: Minimum cover for durability
**Δc_dur,γ**: Safety element modifier
**Δc_dur,st**: Stainless steel reduction factor
**Δc_dur,add**: Additional coating factor
**Δc_dev**: Execution variance allowance (10mm for in-situ, 0-5mm for precast).
standards.notes: Allows adjustments based on manufacturing quality control.

standards.limits:
Mapped by Structural Class (S1-S6) vs Exposure (XC/XD/XS):
• **XC1**: 15mm (S4) / 25mm (S6)
• **XC2/XC3**: 20mm (S4) / 30mm (S6)
• **XC4**: 25mm (S4) / 35mm (S6)
• **XD1/XS1**: 30mm (S4) / 40mm (S6)
• **XD2/XS2**: 35mm (S4) / 45mm (S6)
• **XD3/XS3**: 40mm (S4) / 50mm (S6)
standards.notation:
**c_nom**: Target nominal cover
**c_min**: Max of bond or environmental requirement ($$\max\{c_{min,b}; c_{min,dur}; 10\text{mm}\}$$)
**Δc_dev**: Execution variance safety allowance (standard 10mm).

standards.limits:
• **Cast against earth**: 3 in (75mm)
• **Exposed to weather**: 2 in (50mm) for #6 to #18 bars; 1.5 in (38mm) for #5 or smaller.
• **Not exposed**: Slabs/Walls: 0.75 in (20mm) for ≤#11. Beams/Columns: 1.5 in (38mm).
standards.notation: **No variables available**: Uses static, flat prescriptive table data lookup limits instead.

standards.limits: Calculated dynamically based on target structural reliability index ($$\beta = 1.5\text{ to }3.8$$) mapped against carbonation/chloride diffusion models.
standards.notation:
**P(t)**: Probability of corrosion initiation at time $t$
**c_nom**: Nominal target cover
**x(t)**: Penetration depth of carbonation/chlorides at time $t$
**P_target**: Target reliability threshold.

shell_buckling.title: Steel Shell Buckling Stability
shell_buckling.description: Buckling checks for thin-walled cylindrical or conical steel shells under axial compression, bending, and shear load conditions.
shell_buckling.keywords: steel buckling shell local buckling shell stability imperfection tower cylinder pipe container

standards.limits: Classifies tubular members based on diameter-to-thickness ($$d/t$$) ratios. Semi-compact limit is $$d/t \le 88\epsilon^2$$. Beyond this, the section is slender and requires elastic shell theory check.
standards.notation:
**σ_cr**: Critical local buckling stress capacity
**d / t**: Outer diameter / Wall thickness of shell
**E**: Modulus of elasticity ($$2 \times 10^5\text{ MPa}$$)
**μ**: Poisson's ratio (0.3).
standards.notes: Prescriptive geometric limits for standard pipe profiles.

standards.limits: Evaluates meridional, hoop, and shear stresses against buckling boundaries. Scales capacity via fabrication tolerance classes (Class A, B, or C) using a knock-down factor ($$\chi_{ov}$$).
standards.notation:
**σ_x,Ed / σ_x,Rd**: Design meridional stress / Buckling resistance stress
**χ_x**: Buckling reduction factor from shell slenderness ($$\bar{\lambda}_x$$)
**f_yk**: Yield strength
**γ_M1**: Partial safety factor for buckling resistance.
standards.notes: Rigorous framework linking capacity to fabrication quality tolerances.

standards.clause: Chapter E (Sec E6) & Chapter H (Sec H1.1)
standards.limits: Limits local buckling in round HSS sections via hard geometric boundaries ($$D/t \le 0.45 E / f_y$$). Slender sections exceeding this limit must use reduced critical stress equations.
standards.notation:
**F_cr**: Critical local buckling stress
**D / t**: Outside diameter / Wall thickness
**E / f_y**: Modulus of elasticity ($$29,000\text{ ksi}$$) / Yield stress.
standards.notes: Simple prescriptive lookup for hollow structural sections.

standards.limits: Evaluates shell stability under combined loading interactions via a utilization capacity limit index. Incorporates specialized knock-down curves accounting for large structural shell imperfections.
standards.notation:
**σ_xd, σ_θd, τ_d**: Design axial, hoop, and shear stresses
**σ_cx, σ_cθ, τ_c**: Buckling strengths under isolated loads
**α_1, α_2, α_3**: Code-specified structural interaction exponents.
standards.notes: Industry benchmark standard for heavy thin-walled shell components.

standards.limits: Enforces non-linear numerical safety checks (GNIA - Geometrically Nonlinear Analysis with Imperfections). Maps geometric shell manufacturing deviations directly into finite element models.
standards.notation:
**γ_Rk / E_d**: Global safety factor / Design loading effect matrix
**R_d**: Non-linear resistance structural function
**u / a_init**: Displacements field / Manufacturing imperfection amplitude.

concrete_breakout.title: Anchor Bolt Concrete Breakout Strength
concrete_breakout.description: Calculates the concrete tension breakout capacity of anchor bolt groups embedded in concrete foundation structures.
concrete_breakout.keywords: anchor bolt concrete breakout tension foundation base plate embedment attachment anchor group

standards.limits: Core IS 456 lacks an analytical Concrete Capacity Design (CCD) group model for 35-degree breakout cones. Designs default to verifying simple isolated tensile pullout strength based on concrete tensile capacity across the failure surface area.
standards.notation:
**N_nb**: Nominal concrete breakout tension capacity of the anchor
**f_ct**: Characteristic tensile strength of concrete ($$0.7 \sqrt{f_{ck}}$$)
**A_proj**: Projected effective surface area of the failure cone.
standards.notes: Lacks an explicit group spacing/eccentricity interactive model; international standards typically govern complex configurations.

standards.limits: Uses Concrete Capacity Design (CCD) method. Calculates group breakout capacity in tension assuming a failure prism projecting at 35 degrees.
standards.notation:
**N_cbg**: Nominal breakout strength of an anchor group in tension
**A_Nc / A_Nco**: Actual vs reference single-anchor projected concrete failure area
**ψ_factors**: Group modification factors for eccentricity, edge limits, cracking, and splitting splitting conditions
**N_b**: Basic single anchor breakout capacity ($$k_c \lambda_a \sqrt{f'_c} h_{ef}^{1.5}$$)
**k_c / f'_c / h_ef**: Calibration factor (17 for cast-in) / Compressive strength (psi) / Effective embedment depth.
standards.notes: Standardized group interaction methodology widely integrated in calculation engines.

standards.limits: Applies a standardized CCD group model evaluating actual failure geometry lines under combined tension actions.
standards.notation:
**N_Rk,c**: Characteristic tension breakout group capacity resistance
**N⁰_Rk,c**: Basic single anchor resistance ($$k_1 \sqrt{f_{ck,cube}} h_{ef}^{1.5}$$)
**A_c,N / A⁰_c,N**: Actual vs isolated single projected concrete failure areas
**ψ_factors**: Correction modifiers for edge distance, reinforcement density, and load eccentricity
**k_1 / f_{ck,cube} / h_ef**: Calibration factor (12.7) / Concrete cube strength / Effective embedment depth.
standards.notes: Standard unified calculation format across European jurisdictions.

standards.limits: Evaluates base static breakout capacity ($N_{Rk,c}$) via standard CCD, then scales for fatigue conditions under high-cycle cyclic structural tension repetitions.
standards.notation:
**N_Rk,c**: Characteristic static concrete breakout resistance capacity
**k_1**: Calibration coefficient factor (12.7 for cast-in)
**f_ck / h_ef**: Concrete characteristic cylinder strength / Effective bolt embedment depth
**A_c,N / A⁰_c,N**: Total actual group failure area vs reference single anchor failure area
**ψ_factors**: Group modification factors for edge proximity, structural cracking, and eccentricities.
standards.notes: Comprehensive model accounting for baseline static mechanics and long-term cyclic adjustments.
